meaning: n.撞击,肿块v.碰(伤),撞(破),颠簸
examples: Be careful you don't bump your head.
Small children often cry after a minor bump.
I happened to bump into Mervyn Johns in the hallway.
That's second to Gannett-owned USA Today, which enjoyed a circulation bump of 0.23 percent to 2.28 million.
Just 3% of the "nones" call themselves atheists, a small bump from 2007, when 1.5% did the same.
